<ANS>
1. Standard Scaling:
Apply standard scaling to standardize the values.
 This will ensure that the model's predictions are well-behaved and can be easily interpreted.

2. Handling NaN Values:
For columns with NaN values, imputing them with a suitable value (e.g., mean, median) or using a strategy that preserves the distribution (e.g., median values).
During the inverse transformation, ensure that NaN values are properly handled and re-introduced if necessary.

3. Inverse Transformation:
Implement the inverse_transform method to reverse the scaling.
 This method will be crucial for projecting the model's predictions back to the original domain.
Ensure that the inverse transformation correctly handles the NaN values.
</ANS>
